entry.title: Data Warehouse Concepts
entry.content:
这一章讲什么是Data Warehouse以及基本的概念

A data warehouse is a particular database targeted toward decision support. It takes data from various operational databases and other data sources and transforms it into new structures that fit better for the task of performing business analysis.

Multidimensional Model

传统的database systems：

  • designed and tuned to support the daily operations of an organization
  • primary concern is to ensure fast, concurrent access to data.
  • requires:
    • transaction processing
    • concurrency control capabilities
    • recovery techniques that guarantee data consistency
  • known as operational databases or online transaction processing (OLTP) systems.

传统database system 存在的缺点：

  • perform poorly when executing complex queries that need to join many relational tables together or to aggregate large volumes of data.
  • do not include historical data.

所以需要新的data paradigm –> online analytical processing(OLAP)： analyze the data in organizational databases to support decision making.

因此，一种新的 database model 也随之出现： data warehouse：large repositories that consolidate data from different sources。

data warehouse 也可以用于：reporting, data mining, and statistical analysis.

Data warehouse 和 OLAP 是基于 multidimensional model： views data in an n-dimensional space, usually called a data cube or a hypercube.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 10.57.24](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 10.57.24.png)

A data cube is defined by dimensions and facts：

  • Dimensions：perspectives used to analyze the data. For example, in Fig.3.1, The cube has three dimensions: Product, Time, and Customer.
    • dimension level / data granularity : level of detail at which measures are represented for each dimension of the cube. In the example, sales figures are aggregated to the levels Category, Quarter, and City, respectively.
    • members: Instances of a dimension are called members. For example, Seafood and Beverages are members of the Product dimension at the Category level.
    • attributes: Dimensions also have associated attributes describing them. For example, the Product dimension could contain attributes such as ProductNumber and UnitPrice, which are not shown in the figure.
  • facts: the cells of a data cube, have associated numeric values called measures. each number shown in a cell of the data cube in Fig.3.1 represents a measure Quantity, indicating the number of units sold (in thousands) by category, quarter, and customer’s city.
    • a data cube contains several measures, e.g. Amount, indicating the total sales amount(not shown)

A data cube may be sparse or dense: epending on whether it has measures associated with each combination of dimension values.

dense: all products are bought by all customers during the period of time considered.

sparse: not all customers may have ordered products of all categories during all quarters of the year.

Actually, in real-world applications, cubes are typically sparse.

Hierarchies

An analyst may want to see the sales figures at a finer granularity, such as at the month level, or at a coarser granularity, such as at the customer’s country level.

hierarchies allow viewing data at several granularities:

  • by defining a sequence of mappings relating lower-level, detailed concepts to higher-level, more general concepts.
  • the lower level is called the child
  • the higher level is called the parent.
  • The hierarchical structure of a dimension is called the dimension schema
  • a dimension instance comprises the members at all levels in a dimension.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.11.55](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.11.55.png)

Ex: For the Time dimension, the lowest granularity is Day, which aggregates into Month, which in turn aggregates into Quarter, Semester, and Year.

It is usual to represent the top of the hierarchy with a distinguished level called All.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.14.01](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.14.01.png)

All categories are grouped under a member called all, which is the only member of the distinguished level All.

  • obtaining the aggregation of measures for the whole hierarchy, that is, for obtaining the total sales for all products.

In real-world applications, there exist many kinds of hierarchies.

======== Chapter 4 5========

Measures

Each measure in a cube is associated with an aggregation function that combines several measure values into a single one.

Summarizability: refers to the correct aggregation of cube measures along dimension hierarchies, in order to obtain consistent aggregation results. some conditions may hold:

  • Disjointness of instances: For example, in the hierarchy of Fig. 3.3, a product cannot belong to two categories
  • Completeness: For example, the instances of the Time hierarchy in Fig.3.2 must contain all days in the period of interest, and each day must be assigned to a month.
  • Correctness: correct use of the aggregation functions.

According to the way in which they can be aggregated, measures can be classified as follows:

  • Additive measures summarized along all the dimensions, using addition
  • Semiadditive measures summarized using addition along some, but not all, dimensions.
  • **Nonadditive measures ** cannot be meaningfully summarized using addi- tion across any dimension. Typical examples are item price, cost per unit, and exchange rate.

To avoid computing the whole aggregation from scratch each time the data warehouse is queried, OLAP tools implement incremental aggregation mechanisms.

However incremental aggregation computation is not always possible, since this depends on the kind of aggregation function used. This leads to another classification of measures:

  • Distributive measures: are defined by an aggregation function that can be computed in a distributed way. The usual aggregation functions such as the count, sum, minimum, and maximum are distributive. However, the distinct count function is not.
  • Algebraic measures are defined by an aggregation function that can be expressed as a scalar function of distributive ones.
    • Ex: the average, which can be computed by dividing the sum by the count, the latter two functions being distributive.
  • Holistic measures: measures that cannot be computed from other subaggregates.

OLAP Operations

Ex:

user starts from Fig. 3.4a, a cube containing quarterly sales quantities (in thousands) by product categories and customer cities for the year 2012.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.33.16](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.33.16.png)

user first wants to compute the sales quantities by country. For this, she applies a roll-up operation to the Country level along the Customer dimension.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.33.57](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.33.57.png)

Our user then notices that sales of the category Seafood in France are significantly higher in the first quarter compared to the other ones. Thus, she first takes the cube back to the City aggregation level and then applies a drill-down along the Time dimension to the Month level to find out whether this high value occurred during a particular month.![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.36.16](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.36.16.png)

she sorts the products by name. (a,b,c..p..s)

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.41.47](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.41.47.png)

pivoting the model without changing granularities.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.37.57](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.37.57.png)

she applies a slice operation that results in the subcube to visualize the data only for Paris.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.39.00](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.39.00.png)

user goes back to the original cube and builds a subcube, with the same dimensionality, but only containing sales figures for the first two quarters and for the cities Lyon and Paris. This is done with a dice operation

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.39.09](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.39.09.png)

user now wants to compare the sales quantities in 2012 with those in 2011. For this, she needs the cube in Fig. 3.4h, which has the same structure as the one for 2012

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.39.20](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.39.20.png)

she uses the drill-across operation that, given two cubes, builds a new one with the measures of both in each cell.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.39.35](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.39.35.png)

user now wants to compute the percentage change of sales between the 2 years.She applies to it the add measure operation, which computes a new value for each cell from the values in the original cube.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.39.42](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.39.42.png)

user goes back to the original cube, she first wants to compute to total sales by quarter and city. This is obtained by the sum aggregation operation,

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.42.22](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.42.22.png)

she uses the max operation to obtain the maximum sales by quarter and city

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.54.33](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.54.33.png)

she computes the top two sales by quarter and city, which is also obtained with the max operation

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.12](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.12.png)

the user goes back to the original cube, computes the quarterly sales that amount to 70% of the total sales by city and category. She explores this in two possible ways: according to the ascending order of quarters, as shown in Fig.3.4n, and according to the descending order of quantity, as shown in Fig. 3.4o. In both cases, she applies the top percent aggregation operation.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.19](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.19.png)

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.27](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.27.png)

She also wants to rank the quarterly sales by category and city in descending order of quantity

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.33](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.33.png)

user wants to apply window operations to the cube in Fig. 3.4c. She starts by requesting a 3-month moving average to obtain the result

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.43](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.43.png)

Then, she asks the year-to- date computation

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.50](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.50.png)

user wants to add to the original cube data from Spain, which are contained in another cube. She obtains this by performing a union of the two cubes

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.58](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.40.58.png)

she also wants to remove from the original cube all sales measures except the top two sales by quarter and city. For this, she performs the difference of the original cube in Fig. 3.4a and the cube in Fig. 3.4m, yielding the result in Fig. 3.4t.

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.41.05](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 11.41.05.png)

analogous to the relational algebra operations：

roll-up operation aggregates measures along a dimension hierarchy (using an aggregate function) to obtain measures at a coarser granularity.

ROLLUP(CubeName, (Dimension → Level), AggFunction(Measure))

ROLLUP(Sales, Customer → Country, SUM(Quantity))

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.11.57](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.11.57.png)

Data Warehouse

A data warehouse is defined as a collection of subject-oriented, integrated, nonvolatile, and time-varying data to support management decisions:

  • Subject oriented: data warehouses focus on the analytical needs of different areas of an organization.

  • Integrated: data obtained from several operational and external systems must be joined together

  • Nonvolatile: durability of data is ensured by disallowing data modification and removal, thus expanding the scope of the data to a longer period of time than operational systems usually offer.

  • Time varying: the possibility of retaining different values for the same information, as well as the time when changes to these values occurred.

data marts: a sales department may only need sales data, while a human resources department may need demographic data and data about the employees. These departmental data warehouses are called data marts.

A data warehouse can be seen as a collection of data marts.

bottum-up approach: a data warehouse is built by first building the smaller data marts and then merging these to obtain the data warehouse.

top-down fashion: data marts are obtained from the data warehouse, s sometimes just a logical view of a data warehouse.

Data Warehouse Architecture

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.17.09](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.17.09.png)

  • back-end tier: composed of extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) tools, used to feed data into the data warehouse from
    • operational databases
    • other data sources
      • internal or external from the organization
    • data staging area, which is an intermediate database where all the data integration and transformation processes are run prior to the loading of the data into the data warehouse.
  • data warehouse tier: composed of an enterprise data ware- house and/or several data marts and a metadata repository storing information about the data warehouse and its contents.
  • OLAP tier: composed of an OLAP server, which provides a multidimensional view of the data, regardless of the actual way in which data are stored in the underlying system.
  • front-end tier: used for data analysis and visualization. It contains client tools such as OLAP tools, reporting tools, statistical tools, and data mining tools.

Back-End Tier

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.17.09](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.17.09.png)

ETL process:

  • Extraction:
    • gathers data from multiple, heterogeneous data sources. In order to solve interoperability problems, data are extracted whenever possible using APIs, such as JDBC
  • Transformation: modifies the data from the format of the data sources to the warehouse format.
    • cleaning: removes errors and inconsistencies in the data and converts it into a standardized format
    • integration: reconciles data from different data sources, both at the schema and at the data level
    • aggregation: summarizes the data obtained from data sources according to the level of detail, or granularity, of the data warehouse.
  • Loading: feeds the data warehouse with the transformed data. This also includes refreshing the data warehouse, that is, propagating updates from the data sources to the data warehouse at a specified frequency in order to provide up-to-date data for the decision-making process.

ETL processes usually require a data staging area, that is, a database in which the data extracted from the sources undergoes successive modifications to eventually be ready to be loaded into the data warehouse. Such a database is usually called operational data store.

Data Warehouse Tier

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.17.09](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.17.09.png)

enterprise data warehouse： centralized and encompasses an entire organization

data mart: a specialized data warehouse targeted toward a particular functional or departmental area in an organization.

Metadata: has been tradi- tionally classified into technical and business metadata

  • Business metadata: describes the meaning (or semantics) of the data and organizational rules, policies, and constraints related to the data.
  • technical metadate: describes how data are structured and stored in a computer system and the applications and processes that manipulate such data.

OLAP tier

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.17.09](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.17.09.png)

XMLA (XML for Analysis): aims at providing a common language for exchanging multidimensional data between client applications and OLAP servers.

MDX (MultiDimensional eXpressions): a query language for OLAP databases.

SQL/OLAP: The SQL standard has also been extended for providing analytical capabilities

Front-End Tier

![Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.17.09](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Data_Warehouse/Conceptual_Data_Warehouse/Screenshot 2022-04-18 at 12.17.09.png)

  • OLAP tools: allow interactive exploration and manipulation of the warehouse data
  • Reporting tools: enable the production, delivery, and management of reports, which can be paper-based reports or interactive, web-based reports.
  • Statistical tool: used to analyze and visualize the cube data using statistical methods.
  • Data mining tools: allow users to analyze data in order to discover valuable knowledge such as patterns and trends; they also allow predictions to be made on the basis of current data.

entry.title: Conceptual Data Warehouse Design
entry.content:
这一章主要集中于 conceptual modeling for data warehouse. Conceptual models facilitate communication between users and designers.

Conceptual Modeling of Data Warehouses

conceptual schema: a concise description of the users’ data requirements without taking into account implementation details.

entry.title: Partitioning(数据分区)
entry.content:
Chapter 5 discussed replication. However 面对海量数据或者非常高的查询吞吐量（query throughout）还需要将数据 partitions，或者叫sharding

  • 每个partition可视为一个小的database。
  • a large dataset can be distributed across many disks, and the quey load can be distributed across many processors;

Partitioning and Replication(数据分区和数据复制)

  • a node may store more than one partition

    a leader-follower replication model: Each node may be the leader for some partitions and a follower for other par‐ titions.

![Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 12.06.35](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter6/Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 12.06.35.png)

Partition of Key-Value Data(键-值数据的分区)

how to decide which records to stores on which nodes?

  • assign a continuous range of keys (from some mini‐ mum to some maximum) to each partition, assign each partition a range of hashes (rather than a range of keys), and every key whose hash falls within a partition’s range will be stored in that partition.
  • use a hash function to determine the partition for a given key

skewed: some partitions have more data or queries than others

hot spot: A partition with disproportionately high load

Partitioning by Key Range

assign a continuous range of keys (from some mini‐ mum to some maximum) to each partition,

优点：

  • can easily determine which partition contains a given key
  • make your request directly to the appropriate node

缺点：

  • certain access patterns can lead to hot spots.

Partitioning by Hash of key

use a hash function to determine the partition for a given key， assign each partition a range of hashes (rather than a range of keys), and every key whose hash falls within a partition’s range will be stored in that partition.

![Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 12.22.13](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter6/Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 12.22.13.png)

优点：

  • partition boundaries can be evenly spaced

缺点：

  • lose a nice property of key-range partitioning: the ability to do efficient range queries.

将 Hash of key 和 Range of key 结合：

the first part of that key is hashed to determine the partition, but the other columns are used as a concatenated index for sorting the data

E.X： on a social media site, one user may post many updates. If the primary key for updates is chosen to be (user_id, update_timestamp), then you can efficiently retrieve all updates made by a particular user within some time inter‐ val, sorted by timestamp. Different users may be stored on different partitions, but within each user, the updates are stored ordered by timestamp on a single partition.

Skewed Worloads and Relieving Hot Spots

reads and writes are for the same key, you still end up with all requests being routed to the same partition. 这时候 Hash of Key不能解决hot spots

解决办法：

most data systems are not able to automatically compensate for such a highly skewed workload, so it’s the responsibility of the application to reduce the skew.

App 层面解决： a simple technique is to add a random number to the beginning or end of the key. Just a two-digit decimal random number would split the writes to the key evenly across 100 different keys, allowing those keys to be distributed to different partitions.

Partitioning and Secondary Indexes

  • A secondary index： usually doesn’t identify a record uniquely but rather is a way of searching for occurrences of a particular value: find all actions by user 123, find all articles containing the word hogwash, find all cars whose color is red, and so on.

为什么Secondary Indexes 这么复杂： they don’t map neatly to partitions.

Partitioning Secondary Indexes by Document

each partition is completely separate: each partition main‐ tains its own secondary indexes, covering only the documents in that partition.

![Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 12.35.16](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter6/Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 12.35.16.png)

优点：only need to deal with the partition that contains the document ID that you are writing.

缺点：if you want to search for red cars, you need to send the query to all partitions, and combine all the results you get back.

Partitioning Secondary Indexes by Term

A global index must also be partitioned, but it can be partitioned differently from the primary key index.

![Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 12.38.36](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter6/Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 12.38.36.png)

优点：make reads more efficient: rather than doing scatter/gather over all partitions, a client only needs to make a request to the partition containing the term that it wants.

缺点：writes are slower and more complicated

Rebalancing Partitions

  • rebalancing： The process of moving load from one node in the cluster to another

minimum requirement：

  • After rebalancing, the load (data storage, read and write requests) should be shared fairly between the nodes in the cluster.
  • While rebalancing is happening, the database should continue accepting reads and writes.
  • No more data than necessary should be moved between nodes, to make rebalanc‐ ing fast and to minimize the network and disk I/O load.

Strategies for Rebalancing

How not to do it: hash mod N

The problem with the mod N approach is that if the number of nodes N changes, most of the keys will need to be moved from one node to another.

Fixed number of partitions

![Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 12.46.42](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter6/Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 12.46.42.png)

Only entire partitions are moved between nodes. The number of partitions does not change, nor does the assignment of keys to partitions.

Dynamic partitioning

key range–partitioned databases use Dynamic partitioning, to avoid ll of the data in one partition and all of the other partitions empty

When a partition grows to exceed a configured size (on HBase, the default is 10 GB), it is split into two partitions so that approximately half of the data ends up on each side of the split Conversely, if lots of data is deleted and a partition shrinks below some threshold, it can be merged with an adjacent partition.

优点：the number of partitions adapts to the total data volume.

缺点：starts off with a single partition, since there is no a priori information about where to draw the partition boundaries. While the dataset is small—until it hits the point at which the first partition is split—all writes have to be processed by a single node while the other nodes sit idle.

Partitioning proportionally to nodes

make the number of partitions proportional to the number of nodes—in other words, to have a fixed number of par‐ titions per node

Operations: Automatic or Manual Rebalancing

Fully automated rebalancing：

  • 优点： convenient
  • 缺点：unpredictable

it can be a good thing to have a human in the loop for rebalancing. It’s slower than a fully automatic process, but it can help prevent operational surprises.

Request Routing

Somebody needs to stay on top of those changes in order to answer the question: if I want to read or write the key “foo”, which IP address and port number do I need to connect to?

![Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 13.01.39](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter6/Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 13.01.39.png)

The key problems of those three method is: how does the component making the routing decision.

  1. Many distributed data systems rely on a separate coordination service such as ZooKeeper to keep track of this cluster metadata, as illustrated in Figure 6-8. Each node registers itself in ZooKeeper, and ZooKeeper maintains the authoritative mapping of partitions to nodes.

![Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 13.03.18](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter6/Screenshot 2022-04-22 at 13.03.18.png)

  1. gossip protocol: disseminate any changes in cluster state.

Parallel Query Execution

massively parallel processing (MPP) relational database products, often used for analytics, are much more sophisticated in the types of queries they support.

entry.title: Chapter7 Transaction
entry.content:
Chapter 7 是关于事务，事务将应用程序的多个读，写操作捆绑在一起成为一个逻辑操作单元。要么成功，要么失败

The Slippery Concept of a Transaction

Someone think transaction should be abandoned or redifined the word to describe a much weaker set of guarantees.

Someone think that transaction is an essential requirement

The Meaning of ACID

ACID: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability.

Systems that do not meet the ACID are sometimes called BASE: Basically Available, Soft statem, and Eventual consistency.

Atomicity

In general: something that cannot be broken down into smaller parts.

In computing: in multi-threaded programming, if one thread executes an atomic operation, that means there is no way that another thread could see the half-finished result of the operation. The system can only be in the state it was before the operation or after the operation, not something in between.

In database: if a client wants to make several writes, but a fault occurs after some of the writes have been processed. If the writes are grouped together into an atomic transaction, and the transaction cannot be completed (committed) due to a fault, then the transaction is aborted and the database must discard or undo any writes it has made so far in that transaction.

Consistency

consistency: certain statements about your data (invariants) that must always be true—for example, in an accounting system, credits and debits across all accounts must always be balanced.

The consistency is applied by Application. he application may rely on the database’s atomicity and isolation properties in order to achieve consistency, but it’s not up to the database alone.

Isolation

race conditions: race conditions occur when two threads access a shared variable at the same time. The first thread reads the variable, and the second thread reads the same value from the variable.

Isolation: concurrently executing transactions are isolated from each other: they cannot step on each other’s toes.

Durability

Durability: once a transaction has com‐ mitted successfully, any data it has written will not be forgotten, even if there is a hardware fault or the database crashes.

Single-Object and Multi-Object Operations

![Screenshot 2022-04-25 at 10.24.23](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter7/Screenshot 2022-04-25 at 10.24.23.png)

Isolation prevented that user2 read the middle of the user1 process

![Screenshot 2022-04-25 at 10.25.03](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter7/Screenshot 2022-04-25 at 10.25.03.png)

The atomicity ensures that if update to the counter fails, the transactoin is aborted and the inserted email is rolled back

Single-object writes

The need for multi-object transactions

Handing errors and aborts

Some datastores would abandon the transaction entirely than allow it to remain half-finished.

Some datastores like leaderless replication work much more on “best effort”. At this point, it is the application’s responsibility to recover from errors;

Weak Isolation Levels

serializable isolation means that the database guarantees that transactions have the same effect as if they ran serially

But serializable isolation is expensive, so there are some weaker level of isolation applied.

Read Committed

The most basic level of transaction isolation: read committed:

  • When reading from the database, you will only see data that has been committed( No dirty reads)
  • When writing to the database, you will only overwrite data that has been com‐ mitted(No dirty writes)

No dirty reads

![Screenshot 2022-04-25 at 10.42.35](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter7/Screenshot 2022-04-25 at 10.42.35.png)

dirty read: another transaction see that uncommitted data

No dirty writes

dirty write: earlier write is part of a transaction that has not yet committed, the later write overwrites an uncommitted value

avoids some kinds of concurrency problems:

*

Implementing read committed

prevent dirty write by: using row-level locks

prevent dirty read: hold old and new value, when write transactoin on going, return the old value to read, once the write transaction committed, return the new value;

Snapshot Isolation and Repeatable Read

Snapshot isolation: each transaction reads from a consistent snapshot of the database – that is, the transactions sees all the data that was committed in the database at the start of the trasaction

Implementing snapshot isolation

implementations of snapshot isolation typically use write locks to prevent dirty writes .

For reads, do not need any locks, the key principle of snapshot isolation is: readers never block writers, and writers never block readers.

MVCC: ( multiversion concurrency control) potentially keep several different committed versions of an object

![Screenshot 2022-04-25 at 11.04.07](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Distributed_Sytem/Designing-data-intensive-applications/Chapter7/Screenshot 2022-04-25 at 11.04.07.png)

Visibility rules for observing a consistent snapshot

When a transaction reads from the database, transaction IDs are used to decide which objects it can see and which are invisible.

Indexes and snapshot isolation

Repeatable read and naming confusion

Preventing Lost Updates

lost update problem: application reads some value from the data‐ base, modifies it, and writes back the modified value (a read-modify-write cycle). If two transactions do this concurrently, one of the modifications can be lost, because the second write does not include the first modification.

Atomic write operations

Explicit locking

Automatically detecting lost updates

Compare-and-set

Conflict resolution and replication

multi-leader or leaderless replication may not have up-to-date copy of the data

Write Skew and Phantoms

Characterizing write skew

write skew: two transaction are updating two different objects.

Materializing conflicts

Serializability

Actual Serial Execution

Two-Phase Locking (2PL)

Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI)

entry.title: 01 Static Checking
entry.content:
第一章是关于：

  • static typing
  • the big three properties of good software

Types

A type is a set of values, along with operations that can be performed on those values.

Java type: primitive types are lowercase, while object types start with a capital letter.

  • primitive types:
    • int
    • long
    • boolean
    • double
    • char
  • object types:
    • String
    • BigInteger

Operations: Here are three different syntaxes

  • as an operator: a + b
  • as a method of an object: int_1.add(int_2)
  • as a functoin: Math.add(a,b)

Static typing

  • statically-typed language: types of all variables are known at compile time
  • dynamically-typed languages: this kind of checking is deferred until runtime

static typing is a kind of static checking, which means checking for bugs a compile time, which can catch error while still programming

Support for staitc typing in dynamically-typed lanuages

python3.5 and later allow declare type hints in the code

Checking

  • static checking: error found before the program run
    • syntax error
    • misspelled names
    • wrong number of arguments
    • wrong argument types
    • wrong return types
  • dynamic checking: found when code is executed
    • illegal argument values
    • illegal conversions
    • out-of-range
    • challing a method on a null object
  • no checking: watch error yourself

Surprise: primitive types are not true number

some traps:

  • Integer division: 5 / 2 does not return a fraction
  • Integer overflow: the Integer is bigger then 2 billion
  • Special values in floating-point types: Floating-point types like double have several special values that aren’t real numbers: double/0 will return Infinity

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.34.14](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.34.14.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.34.24](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.34.24.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.34.33](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.34.33.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.34.46](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.34.46.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.34.55](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.34.55.png)

Arrays and collections

  • Arrays:
    • indexing: a[2]
    • assignment: a[2] = 0
    • length: a.length
  • List:
    • indexing: list.get(2)
    • assignment: list.set(2, 0)
    • length: list.size()

Iterating

for loop

Methods

In Java, statements generally have to be inside a method, and every method has to be in a class

Mutating values vs. reassigning variables

final: variables that are assigned once and never reassigned

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.40.33](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.40.33.png)

Documenting assumptions

Programs have to be written with two goals in mind:

  • communicating with the computer.
  • communicating with other people.

Hacking vs. engineering

hacky code:

  • Bad: writing lots of code before testing any of it
  • Bad: keeping all the details in your head, assuming you’ll remember them forever, instead of writing them down in your code
  • Bad: assuming that bugs will be nonexistent or else easy to find and fix

engineers:

  • Good: write a little bit at a time, testing as you go. In a future class, we’ll talk about test-first programming.
  • Good: document the assumptions that your code depends on
  • Good: defend your code against stupidity – especially your own! Static checking helps with that.

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.48.10](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.48.10.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.48.24](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.48.24.png)

The goal of this course

  • safe from bugs: Correctness (correct behavior right now) and defensiveness (correct behavior in the future) are required in any software we build.
  • easy to understand: The code has to communicate to future programmers who need to understand it and make changes in it (fixing bugs or adding new features).
  • ready for change: Software always changes. Some designs make it easy to make changes; others require throwing away and rewriting a lot of code.

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.50.59](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.50.59.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.51.08](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.51.08.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.51.19](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Software_Construction_MIT_6.031/01/Screenshot 2022-05-03 at 13.51.19.png)

entry.title: post
entry.content:
数据仓库的特点：

  • Subject-Oriented

  • Integrated

    ![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.18.45](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.18.45.png)

  • Non-Volatile：

    数据仓库的数据，反应的是一段长时间内历史数据的内容，不同时间点的快照。有大量查询操作，修改和删除操作很少

  • Time-Variant

OLTP： on-line Transaction Processing： RDBMS

OLAP：on-line Analytical Processing： Data Warehouse

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.22.34](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.22.34.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.22.27](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.22.27.png)

!! 实时数据仓库

Data Mart：面向单个部门，只涉及某个主题

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.26.27](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.26.27.png)

每个企业根据自己的业务需求可以分成不同的层次，但是最基本的分层思想：

  • 操作层（ODS）： 临时缓存数据，此层存放未经处理的原始数据至数据仓库系统，结构上与源系统保持一致，是数据仓库的数据准备区，与数据源系统进行解耦合
  • 数据仓库层（DW）：有ODS层数据加工而成，主要完成数据加工与整合。
  • 数据应用层（DA）：提供给产品和数据分析使用的数据

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.27.20](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.27.20.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.28.41](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.28.41.png)

为什么要分层：

  • 清晰数据结构
  • 数据血缘追踪
  • 减少重复开发
  • 把复杂问题简单化
  • 屏蔽原始数据的异常

ETL 是什么？

Extract，Transfer， Load

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.34.48](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.34.48.png)

ETL和ELT的差别

ELT，没有专门的 ODS，意味着数据会立即加载到单一的其中存储库中，数据在数据仓库中进行转换

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.35.22](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.35.22.png)

HIVE

hive将存储在Hadoop文件中结构化和半结构化的文件映射为一张数据库表

hive的核心是将 HQL转换为MapReduce 程序，然后将程序提交到Hadoop集群执行

Hive将文件映射成一张表，映射：映射的专业叫法叫做元数据信息

元数据：

  • table 对应着哪个 文件（位置信息）
  • table 的列对应着文件的哪个字段（顺序信息）
  • 文件字段之间的分隔符是什么

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.47.33](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.47.33.png)

本身承担什么角色？ SQL语法解析MapReduce

Hive架构图

  • 组件：

    • 用户接口： CLI， JDBC/ODBC， WebGUI![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.53.23](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.53.23.png)

    • 元数据存储，通常存储在关系数据库中，mysql/derby。![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.54.03](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.54.03.png)

    • Driver 驱动程序，包括：

      • 语法解析器，
      • 计划编译器
      • 优化器，
      • 执行器

      ![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.54.55](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.54.55.png)

    • 执行引擎

      • MapReduce 默认
      • Tez
      • Spark

      ![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.56.08](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.56.08.png)

Hive的数据模型

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.56.36](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.56.36.png)

Hive有一个默认的根目录，在hive-site.xml 中，由参数 hive.metastore.warehouse.dir 指定。默认值为 /user/hive/warehouse。

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.59.03](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 12.59.03.png)

Partition分区是hive的优化手段。一个文件夹表示一个分区。hive还支持分区下继续创建分区

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 13.00.11](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 13.00.11.png)

Buckets分桶：根据表中字段（ID）的值，经过hash计算，将数据文件划分成指定的若个小文件

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 13.01.56](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 13.01.56.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 13.02.20](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 13.02.20.png)

Hive至适合用来做海量数据的离线分析。Hive不能取代MySQL

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 13.03.44](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 13.03.44.png)

元数据服务：Metastore

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 13.06.37](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 13.06.37.png)

Hive的读写机制

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.36.08](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.36.08.png)

SerDe 是什么？

  • SerDe是Serializer，Deserializer 的简称，目的是用于序列化和反序列化
    • 序列化：对象转化为字节码的过程
    • 反序列化： 字节码转化为对象的过程

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.40.08](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.40.08.png)

![](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.40.17.png)

Hive表默认存储路径是${HIVE_HOME}/conf/hive-site.xml 配置文件中的 hive.metasotre.warehouse 属性决定的，默认是：/user/hive/warehouse

在建表时，通过Location来更改HDFS上的存储路径

  1. 建表

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.49.21](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.49.21.png)

  1. 将local 的文件上传到HDFS，通过映射的方式导入数据。比Insert 快很多

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.49.40](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.49.40.png)

Map 映射。

id ， name， win_rate， skin_price

默认分隔符

hive 默认分隔符是 \001

\001 在文本中显示是 ^A

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.55.28](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.55.28.png)

因此在建表时可以省略 row format![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.55.58](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.55.58.png)

指定存储路径

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.57.52](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 14.57.52.png)

内部表外部表

内部表（Internal table）也称为Hive拥有和管理的托管表（Managed table）。默认情况下创建的表是内部表，Hive拥有该表的结构和文件，换句话说Hive完全管理（元数据和数据）的生命周期，类似于 RDBMS中的表，到删除表时，会删除数据以及表的元数据

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.00.35](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.00.35.png)

外部表

Hive只管理元数据的生命周期。删除表时只删除元数据。在实际中，外部表搭配location语法指定数据的路径， 让数据更安全

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.02.21](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.02.21.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.02.57](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.02.57.png)

Location 关键字

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.05.30](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.05.30.png)

Partitioned Table 分区表

优化，加速查询，减少全表扫描

通过 partitioned by 来创建分区 根据 role_main 的值来分区

如何加载数据进分区表：

  • 传统的的 put 方法会失败

因此要使用静态分区的方法： 分区的属性值时由用户在加载数据的时候手动指定的

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.14.55](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.14.55.png)

Local 参数用于指定待加载的数据是在本地文件系统还是HDFS文件系统

启用hive动态分区，需要在hive会话中设置两个参数：

Bucketed Tables分桶表

将文件分为若干个独立的小文件

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.37.26](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.37.26.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.38.20](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.38.20.png)

分桶的字段要是表中存在字段

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.38.51](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.38.51.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.39.06](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.39.06.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.39.17](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.39.17.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.40.30](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.40.30.png)

分桶表也是需要零时的表，不能直接 put

分桶表的好处：

  • 基于分桶字段查询时，减少全表扫描
  • 在JOIN时，可以提高MR程序效率，较少笛卡尔积数量

![Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.44.57](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/itheima/hive/Screenshot 2022-05-05 at 15.44.57.png)

  • 进行高效抽样

  • 降低查询的复杂度，优化查询语句

Hive 3.0 新特性：Materialized Views物化视图

包括查询结果的数据库对象，可以用于预先计算并保存表

连接或聚集等耗时较多的操作结果。在执行查询时，就可以避免进行这些耗时的操作，从而快速得到结果

表优化设计

Hive查询基本原理

  • 当执行查询计划时，Hive会使用表的最后一级目录作为底层处理数据的输入
  • 然后将整个HDFS中表的目录作为底层查询的输入，可以通过explain命令查看执行计划依赖的数据

Hive表的优化策略：

  • 基于分区表的设计

  • 基于分桶表的设计

  • 索引设计（Hive 3.0 不再支持）

entry.title: post
entry.content:
[toc]

Flink 核心层是 DataStream/DataSet API， 由于新版实现了 流批统一，DataSet API将被弃用

DataStream 本身是Flink的一个用来表示数据集合的类， 我们编写的 Flink 代码其实就是基于这种数据类型的处理。

我们在代码中不关心集合中的具体数据，只是用API 定义出一连串的操作来处理： 数据流的 transformaions

一个 Flink 程序，其实就是对 DataStream 的各种转换

一个Flink 程序由以下几个部分组成：

⚫ 获取执行环境(execution environment)
⚫ 读取数据源(source)
⚫ 定义基于数据的转换操作(transformations)
⚫ 定义计算结果的输出位置(sink)
⚫ 触发程序执行(execute)

![Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 16.29.10](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 16.29.10.png)

  1. Obtain an execution environment,
  2. Load/create the initial data,
  3. Specify transformations on this data,
  4. Specify where to put the results of your computations,
  5. Trigger the program execution

5.1 Obtain an execution environment,

Flink 可以在

  • 本地JVM中运行，
  • 可以在集群上运行

不同的环境，代码的提交运行的过程会有所不同。这就要求我们在提交作业执行计算时， 首先必须获取当前 Flink 的运行环境

5.1.1 创建执行环境

有三种办法创建执行环境

  1. getExecutionEnvironment： 根据当前运行的上下文 直接得到正确的结果
    • 程序是独立运行的，就返回一个本地执行环境
    • 如果是创建了 jar 包，然后从命令行调用它并提交到集群执行，那么就返回集群的执行环境
  2. createLocalEnvironment： 返回本地环境
  3. createRemoteEnvironment： 返回 集群环境
    • createRemoteEnvironment(String host, int port, String… jarFiles)

5.1.2 执行模式

通过不同的执行模式，来决定是，批处理还是流处理

  • Streaming（默认）： 持续实时处理的无界数据流
  • BATCH：批处理， 有界数据
  • AUTOMATIC：由程序根据输入数据源是否有界，来自动选择执行模式。

BATCH模式配置方式：

  1. 命令行
  1. 通过代码: setRuntimeMode

TIP：

不要在代码中配置，而是使用命令行, 这样更加灵活，而不是使用hard code

5.2 Load/create the initial data,

一般将数据的输入来源称为数据源（data source）， 而读取数据的算子就是 source operator

Flink 代码中，通用的添加 source 的方式，是调用 addSource() 方式

5.2.1 从文件中读取

readTextFile(path)

  • ⚫ 参数可以是目录，也可以是文件;

  • ⚫ 路径可以是相对路径，也可以是绝对路径;

  • ⚫ 相对路径是从系统属性user.dir获取路径: idea下是project的根目录, standalone模式下是集群节点根目录;

  • ⚫ 也可以从 hdfs 目录下读取, 使用路径 hdfs://…, 由于 Flink 没有提供 hadoop 相关依赖,

    需要 pom 中添加相关依赖:

5.2.2 Collection 中读取

  • fromCollection(Collection)

  • fromCollection(Iterator, Class)

  • fromElements(T …)

5.2.3 从Socket 中读取

socketTextStream

这种方式由于吞吐量小、稳 定性较差，一般也是用于测试。

5.2.4 自定义addSource： 例 –> 从Kafka读取

![Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 16.38.57](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 16.38.57.png)

Flink 内部并没有提供预实现的方法。所以我 们只能采用通用的 addSource 方式、实现一个 SourceFunction.

  1. Flink 官方提供了连接工具 flink-connector-kafka， 直接帮我们实现了一个消费者 FlinkKafkaConsumer，它就是用来读取 Kafka 数据的 SourceFunction.

    导入依赖

  2. addSource

    addSource 方法传入一个对象参数，需要实现 SourceFunction 接口， 这里， FlinkKafkaConsumer 是一个 source function ，实现了 SourceFunction 接口

![Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 16.50.07](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 16.50.07.png)

5.2.5 自定义 Source

自定义 Data Source，主要重写两个方法： run() and cancel()

  • run()方法:使用运行时上下文对象(SourceContext)向下游发送数据;
  • cancel()方法:通过标识位控制退出循环，来达到中断数据源的效果。

调用：

5.2.5.1 完整的一个Flink程序

5.3 Flink 支持的数据类型

5.3.1 TypeInfomation

用水流来类比，那么我们要处理的数据元素就是随着水流漂动 的物体。在这条流动的河里，可能漂浮着小木块，也可能行驶着内部错综复杂的大船。要分布 式地处理这些数据，就不可避免地要面对数据的网络传输、状态的落盘和故障恢复等问题，这 就需要对数据进行序列化和反序列化。小木块是容易序列化的;而大船想要序列化之后传输， 就需要将它拆解、清晰地知道其中每一个零件的类型。

为了方便处理数据， Flink 用 TypeInfomation 来统一表示数据类型

TypeInformation 类是 Flink 中所有类型描述符的基类。 它涵盖了类型的一些基本属性，并为每个数据类型生成特定的序列化器、反序列化器和比较器。

5.3.2 Flink 支持的数据类型：

  1. 基本类

    1. 所有 Java 基本类型及其包装类，再加上 Void、String、Date、BigDecimal 和 BigInteger。

  2. 数据类

    1. 基本类型数组(PRIMITIVE_ARRAY)和对象数组(OBJECT_ARRAY)

  3. 复合数据类型

    1. Java 元组类型(TUPLE):这是 Flink 内置的元组类型，是 Java API 的一部分。最多

      25 个字段，也就是从 Tuple0~Tuple25，不支持空字段

    2. Scala 样例类及 Scala 元组:不支持空字段

    3. 行类型(ROW):可以认为是具有任意个字段的元组,并支持空字段

    4. POJO:Flink 自定义的类似于 Java bean 模式的类（ 在项目实践中，往往会将流处理程序中的元素类型定为 Flink 的 POJO 类型。）

      Flink 对 POJO 类型的要求：

      • 类是公共的(public)和独立的(standalone，也就是说没有非静态的内部类);

      • 类有一个公共的无参构造方法;

      • 类中的所有字段是public且非final的;或者有一个公共的getter和setter方法，这些

        方法需要符合 Java bean 的命名规范。

  4. 辅助类型

    1. Option、Either、List、Map 等

  5. 泛型类型(GENERIC)

    1. Flink 会把泛型类型当作黑盒，无法获取它们内部的属性;它 们也不是由 Flink 本身序列化的，而是由 Kryo 序列化的。

5.3.3 Type Hints

存在问题： 分析函数的输入和返回类型，自动获取类型信息，从而获得对应的序列化器和反序列化器。但是，由于 Java 中泛型擦除的存在，在某些特殊情 况下(比如Lambda表达式中)，自动提取的信息是不够精细的——只告诉Flink当前的元素由 “船头、船身、船尾”构成，根本无法重建出“大船”的模样;这时就需要显式地提供类型信 息，才能使应用程序正常工作或提高其性能。

为了解决这类问题，Java API 提供了专门的“类型提示”(type hints)。

  2. 传入匿名类

第 1 2 种方法，需要重写 map() 方法，定义从一个输入事件转换为另一个输出事件的具体逻辑。 需要指定两个类型，分别是 输入输出事件的类型 new MapFunction<Event, Object>() 。

源码部分：

基于 DataStream 调用 map 方法，返回 的其实是一个 SingleOutputStreamOperator

![Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 17.24.59](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 17.24.59.png)

5.4.1.2 filter

![Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 17.26.13](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 17.26.13.png)

对数据流执行一个过滤，通过一个布尔条件表达式设置过滤 条件，对于每一个流内元素进行判断，若为 true 则元素正常输出，若为 false 则元素被过滤掉

filter 转化需要参数，实现FilterFunction 接口

  1. FilterFunction 实现类
  1. 匿名内部类

5.4.1.3 flatmap

![Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 17.31.44](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 17.31.44.png)

将数据流中的整体(一般是集合类型)拆分成一个 一个的个体使用。消费一个元素，可以产生 0 到多个元素。flatMap 可以认为是“扁平化(”flatten) 和“映射”(map)两步操作的结合，也就是先按照某种规则对数据进行打散拆分，再对拆分 后的元素做转换处理

  1. FlatMap接口实现类
  1. 匿名内部类

  1. lambda 表达式

flatMap 会返回0 个、1 个或多个 结果数据， 因此flatMap 并没有直接定义返回值类型，而是通过一个“收集器”(Collector)来 指定输出。

5.4.2 聚合算子（Aggregation）

结果不仅依赖当前数据，还跟之前的数据有关，相当于要把所有数据聚在一起进行汇总合并 ——这就是所谓的“聚合”(Aggregation)，也对应着 MapReduce 中的 reduce 操作

5.4.2.1 keyBy

在 Flink 中，要做聚合，需要先进行分区，这个操作就是通过 keyBy 来完成的。

![Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 17.49.47](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 17.49.47.png)

keyBy 是聚合前必须要用到的一个算子。keyBy 通过指定键(key)，可以将一条流从逻辑 上划分成不同的分区(partitions)。这里所说的分区，其实就是并行处理的子任务，也就对应 着任务槽(task slot)。

在内部，是通过计算key的hash code 来分区的，如果是pojo 则需重写 hashCode方法。

keyBy 参数：

  1. 对于 Tuple 数据类型，可以指定字段的位置或者多个位置的组合
  2. 对于 POJO 类 型，可以指定字段的名称(String)
  3. 可以传入 Lambda 表达式
  4. 实现一个键选择器(KeySelector)

返回的不是 DataStream，是KeyStream，但是继承DataStream

![Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 18.02.57](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-09 at 18.02.57.png)

只有基于 KeyStream 才可以做后续的聚合操 作(比如 sum，reduce); 而且它可以将当前算子任务的状态(state)也按照 key 进行划分、限 定为仅对当前 key 有效。

5.4.2.2 简单聚合

  • ⚫ sum():在输入流上，对指定的字段做叠加求和的操作。

  • ⚫ min():在输入流上，对指定的字段求最小值。

  • ⚫ max():在输入流上，对指定的字段求最大值。

  • ⚫ minBy():与 min()类似，在输入流上针对指定字段求最小值。不同的是，min()只计

    算指定字段的最小值，其他字段会保留最初第一个数据的值;而 minBy()则会返回包含字段最小值的整条数据。

  • ⚫ maxBy():与 max()类似，在输入流上针对指定字段求最大值。两者区别与min()/minBy()完全一致。

简单聚合算子返回的，同样是一个 SingleOutputStreamOperator，也就是从 KeyedStream 又 转换成了常规的 DataStream。

一个聚合算子，会为每一个 key 保存一个聚合的值，在 Flink 中我们把它叫作“状态”(state)。 所以每当有一个新的数据输入，算子就会更新保存的聚合结果，并发送一个带有更新后聚合值 的事件到下游算子

5.4.3.3 富函数（Rich Fucntion Classes）

所有的Flink函数都有其Rich的版本。比常规的函数类提供更多、更丰富的功能。

富函数特点：可以获取运行环境的上下文，并拥有一些生命周期方法，所以可以实现 更复杂的功能

Rich Function有生命周期的概念：

  • open（）： 初始化时被调用
  • close（）： 最后被调用

对于一个并行子任务来说只会调用一次：例如 RichMapFunction 中的 map()，在每条数据到来后都会触发一次调用。

常见的场景：

在 open（） 中连接数据库，在map（）中读写数据，在close（）中关闭连接

富函数提供了：

getRuntimeContext()方法， 可以获取到运行时上下文的一些信息，例如

  • 程序执行的并行度，
  • 任务名
  • 状态 (state)。

5.4.4 物理分区（Physical Partitioning）

keyBy是一种逻辑分区（软分区）： 保证把数据按 key“分开”，至于分得均不均匀、每个 key 的数据具体会分到哪一区去，这些是完全无从控制 的

物理分区：精准地调配数据，告诉每个数据到底去哪里

分区算子 并不对数据进行转换处理，只是定义了数据的传输方式

5.4.4.1 随机分区（shuffle）

![Screenshot 2022-05-10 at 15.24.32](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-10 at 15.24.32.png)

随机分区服从 （uniform distribution）

5.4.4.2 轮询分区（Round-Robin）

![Screenshot 2022-05-10 at 15.26.02](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-10 at 15.26.02.png)

按照先后顺序将数据做依次分发，使用的是 Round-Robin 算法（该算法还用在 Kafka，Nginx中）

5.4.4.3 重缩放分区（rescale）

与 轮询分区相似，只是将 数据轮询发送到下游并行任务的一部分中。

当下游任务(数据接收方)的数量是上游任务(数据发送方)数量的整数倍时，rescale 的效率明显会更高。比如当上游任务数量是 2，下游任务数量是 6 时，上游任务其中一个分区 的数据就将会平均分配到下游任务的 3 个分区中。

5.4.4.4 广播（broadcast）

，数据会在不同的分区都保留一 份，可能进行重复处理

5.4.4.5 全局分区（global）

将所有的输入流数据都发送到下游算子的第一个并行子任务中去。强行让下游的并行度变成了1

5.4.4.6 自定义分区（Custom）

调用时，要传入两个参数：

  1. 自定义分区器(Partitioner)对象
  1. 应用分区器的字段,它的指定方式与 keyBy 指定 key 基本一样
    • 通过字段名称指定
    • 通过字段位置索引
    • 实现一个KeySelector

5.5.1 连接到外部系统

Flink对于稳定性和容错性要求极高，一旦出现故障，要有恢复到之前状态的能力。Flink内部提供 checkpoint，保障我们可以回滚到正确的状态。但如果我们在处理过程中任意读写外部系统，发生故障后 就很难回退到从前了。

为了避免以上的问题，Flink 提供了addSink： 用来实现与外 部系统连接、并将数据提交写入

print就是一种输出算子

![Screenshot 2022-05-10 at 15.43.38](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-10 at 15.43.38.png)

与Source算子很像。除去一些Flink预实现的Sink，一般情况下的Sink算创建是调用 addSink（）

addSink 要重写一些 invoke （）方法

Flink提供：

![Screenshot 2022-05-10 at 15.45.53](/Users/zhangyuyang/Desktop/blog/source/_posts/Flink/Chapter05/Screenshot 2022-05-10 at 15.45.53.png)

5.5.2 输出到文件

简单粗暴的预实现方式 ：writeAsText()、writeAsCsv()， 但是这些方法不支持同时写入。

因此Flink专门提供了一个流文件系统的 连接器：StreamingFileSink

通过.withRollingPolicy()方法指定了一个“滚动策略”:什么时候就开启新的文件，将之前的内容归档保存。

⚫ 至少包含15分钟的数据

⚫ 最近5分钟没有收到新的数据

⚫ 文件大小已达到 1 GB
